metadata.description: Deploys an Azure Container Instance running a custom PowerShell script that sends synthetic Spam/Phish/Malware emails via SMTP relay to EOP. Supports custom sender, subjects, attachments, and phishing URLs. Optionally deploys a NAT Gateway for static egress IP control.
EOP MX endpoint hostname, e.g. 'contoso-com.mail.protection.outlook.com'. Find via: (Resolve-DnsName -Name <tenantname>.onmicrosoft.com -Type MX).NameExchange
SMTP port. Use 25 for direct-to-MX (no auth) or 587 for SMTP submission (STARTTLS).
Comma-separated list of sender email addresses. A random sender is chosen for each round of emails.
Comma-separated list of recipient email addresses.
Pipe-separated list of custom subject lines (e.g. 'Urgent: Verify Account|Invoice Attached|Meeting Update|Action Required'). If empty, random subjects are generated from fallback text.

Pipe-separated list of URLs to download attachment files from (e.g. 'https://example.com/report.pdf|https://example.com/data.xlsx'). Files are downloaded at startup and randomly attached to emails. Leave empty for no custom attachments.
Pipe-separated list of phishing test URLs to embed in phish-type emails. These trigger Safe Links detection.
Number of emails each recipient should receive. The script sends in rounds of 5 random recipients until all have reached this count.
Percentage of emails that include a threat payload: GTUBE (spam), phishing URL, or EICAR (malware) attachment (0-100).
If true, deploys a NAT Gateway with a static public IP for controlled egress. If false, the container uses a random Azure public IP.
Address prefix for the VNet and subnet (only used when deployNatGateway is true).

properties.description: Allow SMTP submission (STARTTLS) outbound to EOP
